predictor: Myocardial enzymes (LDH, CK, CK-MB, troponin) and clinical characteristics integrated into a nomogram
standardized_predictor: Myocardial enzymes (LDH, CK, CK-MB, troponin) and clinical characteristics integrated into a nomogram
method: Multivariate logistic regression, ROC analysis, calibration curve, decision curve analysis; compared with LDH alone, SOFA, and SAPS II
notes: Data extracted from MIMIC-III database; training/testing split 7:3; univariate logistic regression used for variable screening followed by stepwise regression

performance: AUC = 0.773 (training set, 95% CI: 0.748–0.798); AUC = 0.750 (testing set, 95% CI: 0.711–0.789) for LDH-model
notes: LDH alone was inferior to the LDH-model nomogram. LDH-model also outperformed SOFA score and SAPS II score. Calibration curve showed good agreement; decision curve analysis demonstrated clinical utility.
Model superior to LDH alone, SOFA score, and SAPS II score. Calibration curve indicated good agreement between predictive and actual outcomes; decision curve analysis demonstrated clinical utility.

population: Patients with sepsis in the ICU (MIMIC-III database)
timing: At ICU admission or during ICU stay
notes: Study purpose stated; actual statistical results not provided in this excerpt. The study aimed to investigate the relationship between myocardial enzymes and one-year mortality and to establish a prediction model using MIMIC-III database.

population: Sepsis patients in ICU aged ≥18 years with ≥24h ICU admission and ≥1 day survival time
sample_size: N=1,983 (training set n=1,388; testing set n=595)
notes: Mean age 67.79 ± 15.31 years; 46.44% had congestive heart failure; 26.42% had history of malignant disease. No primary experimental findings with statistical results reported in this excerpt.

population: Sepsis patients from a database, split into training and testing sets
sample_size: N=1388 total (training set: death group n=788, survival group n=600)
predictor: Congestive heart failure, malignant tumor, atrial fibrillation, respiratory failure, Elixhauser score
standardized_predictor: Congestive heart failure, malignant tumor, atrial fibrillation, respiratory failure, Elixhauser score
method: Comparison between death and survival groups
performance: Differences statistically significant between death and survival groups (P not specified for individual variables)
notes: One-year mortality rate was 56.13%. Median Elixhauser score was 24.00 for total population. 42.16% had atrial fibrillation. Training and testing sets were well-balanced (all variables P>0.05 between sets).
AST, CK, and CK-MB were not significantly associated with one-year mortality in sepsis patients.

source_info.section: **The association of different myocardial enzymes and one‑year mortality among patients with sepsis**

notes: No statistically significant difference found between AST and one-year mortality.

notes: No statistically significant difference found between CK and one-year mortality.

source_info.section: **The association of different myocardial enzymes and one‑year mortality among patients with sepsis**

outcome: Not reported (mortality or adverse outcome implied)
standardized_outcome: Not reported (mortality or adverse outcome implied)
notes: LDH-model exhibited superior predictive performance compared to LDH alone, SOFA score, and SAPS II score in both training and testing sets. DCA curves also showed higher clinical benefit for LDH-model under the same risk threshold.
An online prediction nomogram was developed and is available at https://songqy.shinyapps.io/DynNomapp/

method: Nomogram prediction model with internal validation; compared against LDH alone, SOFA score, and SAPS II score
performance: Good performance on internal validation (specific metrics not reported in excerpt)
notes: Model integrates LDH with 11 clinical features. Compared favorably to LDH alone, SOFA score, and SAPS II score. Example patient: male, age 81, LDH 371 IU/L, potassium 4.8 mEq/L, calcium 11.1 mg/dL, albumin 4 g/dL, hemoglobin 8.9 g/dL, ALP 159 IU/L, no vasopressor, Elixhauser score 42.

performance: Not reported (used as comparator; LDH-model outperformed LDH alone)
notes: LDH was independently associated with risk of one-year mortality in sepsis patients.

LDH level was found to be associated with the risk of one-year mortality among patients with sepsis. A nomogram was developed incorporating LDH and ALP (alkaline phosphatase) for predicting one-year mortality probability.
ALP was included in the nomogram predicting one-year mortality probability in patients with sepsis.
Retrospective cohort study exploring association of myocardial enzymes and prognosis of sepsis. Full performance metrics not provided in this excerpt.

predictor: LDH-model (integrating LDH and clinical features including ALP)
standardized_predictor: LDH-model (integrating LDH and clinical features including ALP)
method: ROC analysis, calibration curves, DCA curves, nomogram
notes: LDH-model compared with SAPS II, SOFA scoring systems and LDH alone for one-year prediction of septic mortality; nomogram developed for clinical application
Used as comparator to LDH-model
Used as comparator to LDH-model
Used as comparator to LDH-model
LDH was associated with one-year mortality risk in sepsis patients

predictor: LDH-based nomogram (LDHmodel) combining LDH and clinical characteristics
standardized_predictor: LDH-based nomogram (LDHmodel) combining LDH and clinical characteristics
performance: Surpassed predictive ability of LDH alone, SAPS II, and SOFA scoring systems
notes: Nomogram developed to assist clinicians in predicting one-year mortality risk
LDH-model is a composite prediction model including LDH among other variables
SOFA: Sequential Organ Failure Assessment score
SAPS II: Simplified Acute Physiology Score II
LDH: Lactic dehydrogenase measured alone as a single predictor

performance: Training set AUC: 0.606 (0.560–0.642) to 0.672 (0.528–0.636) range across metrics; Testing set AUC: 0.536 (0.496–0.576) to 0.581 (0.525–0.636) range across metrics
notes: LDH-model is a predicting model integrating LDH and clinical features. A nomogram was developed for clinical decision-making. External validation cohort noted as needed for future studies.
